parsed_review.summary: Independent read-only review of CP00-263 (RP07 search-core, risk A, 10 units, RP07.P07.M05.S21-RP07.P07.M06.S05: Permission And Audit Binding tail + Synthetic Fixture Set head). The pack is a clean descriptor-only continuation of the established RP07 pattern and its claims are supported. Verified: (1) identity/coverage agree across manifest, review-baseline, live closeout-pack-plan.json (CP00-263 at line 412), and contract current_pack — 10 units, deliverable split {claude_review:1, implementation:4, failure_recovery:5}; (2) the generated descriptor is deep-equaled against the static contract block (validate script lines 1394-1400) with cp263_requirements/cp263_no_write_attestation also deep-equaled, giving strong drift protection; M05/M06 row extras resolve correctly through the CP261->CP260 cumulative layers (missing_* => rejected_customer_safe, failure_taxonomy validation_error_detail_included=false); (3) no-write/no-real-data boundaries hold — every dispatches_*/writes_*/evaluates_runtime_permission/emits_hermes_runtime_receipt/executes_command_runtime/loads_real_fixture_data is false, synthetic_only and no_real_data true, implementation is frozen-object factories only, git diff --check exit 0; (4) Claude stays a read-only reviewer (claude_is_final_approval=false, read_only=true, tools=Read/Grep/Glob, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false); (5) CP178+ hardening blockers are encoded (auth failure, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped, disabled tools, missing refs, final-approval/enterprise-trust claims) and the readiness auth-preflight returned valid JSON; (6) Hermes appears only as a deterministic descriptor evidence packet (H07, receipt_shape descriptor_only), with no_hermes_runtime_receipt true and no product-runtime embedding. The manifest honestly reports production_ready=false / implemented_pending_review with pack-level review pending; absent post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are the expected pre-review state gated on this review, not a defect. One non-blocking P3 naming observation; no P0/P1/P2 findings. Final enterprise-trust/runtime opening remains gated on human approval (CP00-264), consistent with the authority boundary.

findings.title: CP263 case-set rows sourced from CP261-named cumulative extras constant (naming/readability only)
findings.details: createSearchCoreCp263FailureBindingTailCaseSet builds row metadata by spreading SEARCH_CORE_CP261_ROW_EXTRAS[key] (service.js:3815-3835), and that constant in turn spreads SEARCH_CORE_CP260_ROW_EXTRAS (service.js:3560-3566). For CP00-263 this resolves correctly: the M05 tail keys (claude_edge_case_prompt, human_escalation_note, no_silent_success_check, no_data_leak_check) come from the CP261 layer and the M06 head keys (failure_taxonomy, missing_tenant_failure, missing_actor_failure, missing_matter_failure, missing_resource_failure) come from the CP260 layer (service.js:3414-3418). Correctness is independently confirmed because the validator deep-equals the generated descriptor against the static contract failure_binding_tail_descriptor (scripts/validate-rp07-search-core-contract.mjs:1394-1398) and that block (contracts/search-core-contract.json:30752-30811) carries the expected per-row extras (missing_* => expected_outcome rejected_customer_safe; failure_taxonomy => validation_error_detail_included false). The observation is purely that a CP261-named constant is the live source for CP263 rows, which a future maintainer extending the chain could find non-obvious; there is no functional impact and no drift.
findings.recommendation: No action required for CP00-263 closeout. Optionally, in a future cleanup, introduce a per-pack alias (e.g. SEARCH_CORE_CP263_ROW_EXTRAS) or a short comment documenting the intentional cumulative reuse so the row-key->extras source is self-evident.
findings.post_review_disposition: Acceptable as-is; verified correct for this pack via contract deep-equal. Non-blocking; informational only.
